preamble: The shift from the voiced bilabial stop [b] to the voiced labio-velar approximant [w] is a form of lenition. It involves the weakening of the full labial closure into a glide, frequently observed in intervocalic positions where articulation is less energetic.

examples.note: Intervocalic /b/ spirantizes to [β], which often approaches a labiovelar glide [w] in rapid speech.

sources: Penny, R. (2002). A History of the Spanish Language.
Ladefoged, P. (2001). A Course in Phonetics.
